source: Border Town Burning (unofficial Mordheim supplement), fetched from https://broheim.net/downloads/campaigns/bordertownburning/Border%20Town%20Burning.pdf on 2026-07-03 (Objectives chapter, book p.39-45 / Dramatis Personae chapter, book p.176-186). Owner should cross-check against their own BTB PDF/printing for errata. List is NOT exhaustive for the full BTB book — only the Dramatis Personae chapter's named/templated characters found in the fetched text extraction are included (Ippan Shu, Maglah Khan's Horde, Belandysh, and the Daemon Prince ascension template). TODO: verify vs the BTB book for any Dramatis Personae outside this chapter.

characters.name: Grand Master Ippan Shu
characters.hireFee: 75 gold crowns and 3 campaign points to hire
characters.mayBeHiredBy: Any warband which includes Humans or Elves, including Battle Monks, EXCLUDING Dark Elves, Outlaws and Bandits.
characters.equipment: An iron fan in one hand; fights unarmed with the other.
skills: all Battle Monk special skills except Warmonger
characters.specialRules: Bare-handed Fighting: fights unarmed with no penalty, counting as having a second close combat weapon for +1 Attack. Iron Fan: +1 Initiative; parries not only blows but missile shots (parries a missile hit if he beats the to-hit roll; also has Dodge). Immune to Psychology: automatically passes all Leadership-based tests. With the Elegance of a Feather: may move up to half his total movement (6") in any direction when running/charging, including off the ground (not just horizontally), combinable with Leap for up to 6+D6"; must still finish movement on solid ground. Way of the Dragon: in the shooting phase, breathes fire using the flame template for an automatic S4 hit, plus a 4+ chance to set targets on fire; usable even while in close combat.

characters.name: Maglah Khan's Horde
characters.mayBeHiredBy: Orcs & Goblins, Ogres, Chaos Dwarfs, Marauders of Chaos, Norse, Beastmen, Possessed and Mercenaries. NOTE: "Ogres" here plausibly includes Maneaters warbands — TODO verify this interpretation against the BTB Maneaters entry / errata, as the Maneaters warband PDF itself (Border Town Burning — Maneaters.pdf) does not mention Maglah Khan.
characters.equipment: Spear, sword, bow, shield, heavy armour and helmet. Rides a Giant Wolf (M9 WS3 BS0 S3 T3 W1 I4 A1 Ld4).
characters.specialRules: Maglah's Boyz: cannot be hired alone — the warband must also hire 2-5 Hobgoblin Scout Hired Swords, all of whom (except one) leave if Maglah leaves. Hobgoblin leader: Maglah is the leader of all Hobgoblin Scouts in the warband; any within 6" may use his Leadership for Ld tests (not the warband's own leader's Leadership).

characters.name: Belandysh, Condemned Champion of Chen
characters.hireFee: 90 gold crowns and 5 campaign points to hire
characters.mayBeHiredBy: Marauders of Chaos, Beastmen, Norse and Possessed.
characters.equipment: The Broadsword of Damnation (a Chaos Artefact). Wears a Helmet and a Chaos Armour barely holding his mutated body together.
characters.specialRules: Profile is variable (M4, WS/S/T/I roll D6 each, A rolls D3, Ld10) due to the Inconsistency special rule — re-rolled once per turn as needed; NOT a fixed stat line, left null here pending a proper representation in the app. Mount Tol'Agath acts as a Warhorse (M8 WS3 BS0 S-D6 T-D6 W1 I3 A1 Ld5) with the Battle Schooled rule, also subject to Inconsistency. Immune to psychology. Regeneration: on a D6 of 4+, ignores a wound taken (not wounds from fire/fire magic). Wrath of Tchar: if Belandysh is taken out of action in a battle where an enemy picks up the Broadsword of Damnation, he is pulled into the Realm of Chaos and cannot be hired again for the rest of the campaign.
characters.notes: book p.178. TODO: the app's NullableStatLine model assumes fixed numeric stats — Belandysh's Inconsistency mechanic (several characteristics re-rolled on D6/D3 every turn) doesn't fit that shape and needs a special-cased UI/data representation later.

characters.name: Daemon Prince (Scion of Chaos ascension)
characters.hireFee: 200 gold crowns and D6 campaign points to hire (his original warband is exempt from upkeep)
characters.mayBeHiredBy: Only warbands with the Scion of Chaos objective — and only after a warband's own leader has ascended into this role per the objective's 35 CP 'Daemon Prince' achievement (see btb/objectives.json, scionOfChaos).
characters.equipment: The Broadsword of Damnation, Chaos Dragon Scale Armour, Ghartok's Skull, and the Liber Chaotica (all Chaos Artefacts), plus whatever equipment the ascending Hero already carried.
characters.specialRules: This is a BLANK CHARACTER TEMPLATE in the source book (the printed page has underscored blanks for profile/equipment/skills, to be filled in with the ascended leader's own values) rather than a fixed named character — statLine, equipment and skills are intentionally left null/empty here. Leader: automatically becomes the leader of any warband he joins; any warrior within 6" may use his Leadership. Mark of Chaos: has the Mark of Chaos as per the Marauders of Chaos warband. Immune to poison. Immune to psychology (auto-passes all Leadership/Rout tests). Cause Fear. Daemonic aura: 5+ armour save, negated by magic weapons/spells; his attacks count as magical. Flying: triples Movement when running, ignores terrain. Chosen Servant of Chaos: does not automatically drop the Chaos Artefacts when taken out of action — roll Serious Injuries immediately; if he dies, place markers for the artefacts instead.
